All States · Mississippi

Mississippi Dietitian License

Professional Licensure - Dietetics. $100 application. 3 to 4 Weeks processing.

Last verified 2026-02-25 · Board-sourced data
↗ Official Board Portal (opens in a new tab)

Quick Facts at a Glance

Application Fee
$100
$100 initial application and licensure fee
Processing
3 to 4 Weeks
Subject to board volume
Renewal
$100
Every 2 years (by March 31 of odd years) + 30 CE hours
License Duration
2 Years
From date of issuance
Licensing Authority

Professional Licensure - Dietetics

Mississippi State Department of Health
Phone
(601) 364-7360
Email
ProfLicensure@msdh.ms.gov
Mailing Address
P.O. Box 1700
Jackson, MS 39215-1700
Physical Address
143B Lefleur's Square
Jackson, MS 39211
Credential Categories

Mississippi Dietitian License Types

Licensed Dietitian (LD)

The credential to practice dietetics in Mississippi, overseen by the Mississippi State Department of Health.

Provisional Licensed Dietitian

Available for those who have completed their education and supervised practice but have not yet passed the CDR exam. Valid for one year, costs $50, and requires practice under the supervision of a Licensed Dietitian.

Application Process

How to Get Licensed in Mississippi

Complete Education & Supervised Practice

Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete a minimum of 900 hours of board-approved supervised practice through an ACEND-accredited dietitian internship.

Pass the CDR Examination

Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians. Verification of current RD status is required.

Pass the Jurisprudence Exam

All applicants must successfully complete an examination testing their knowledge of Mississippi's dietetic statutes and administrative rules.

Submit the Application via LEMS

Create an account on Mississippi's Licensure and Enforcement Management System (LEMS) portal. Complete the application and pay the $100 fee.

Required Documents

Application Checklist

  • ✓
    CDR Registration Verification Proof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
  • ✓
    Jurisprudence Exam Score Documentation of successfully passing the state-specific rules exam.
  • ✓
    Official College Transcripts Sent directly from the university showing your conferred degree.
Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Provisional Rules
Mississippi offers a provisional license ($50 fee) for individuals who have completed the academic and supervised practice requirements but have not yet passed the CDR exam. A Provisional Licensed Dietitian must practice under the direct supervision of an active Mississippi Licensed Dietitian. The provisional license is valid for one year and can be renewed annually for an additional $50.
Reciprocity & Endorsement
Mississippi allows for licensure by reciprocity (endorsement) for applicants holding a current, active license in another state with requirements substantially equivalent to Mississippi's, provided the out-of-state license is in good standing.
Travel Dietitian Timeline
If your assignment start date is within 5 weeks, apply immediately and call the board at (601) 364-7360 to confirm current processing times.
Maintenance

Renewal Requirements

Mississippi dietitian licenses renew every 2 years (by march 31 of odd years) + 30 ce hours. Renewal fee is $100.

Frequently Asked Questions

Mississippi Dietitian FAQ

How much does a Mississippi dietitian license cost?

The initial license application fee is a non-refundable $100. The provisional license costs $50.

When does the Mississippi license need to be renewed?

Mississippi regular dietitian licenses are renewed biennially (every two years) by March 31 of odd-numbered years. The renewal fee is $100.

Does Mississippi have state-specific continuing education requirements?

Yes. Regular license holders must complete 30 hours of approved continuing education every two years. Provisional license holders must complete 15 hours annually.
